com.hp.workpath.api.job

Class JobService

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hp.workpath.api.job.JobService

  • public class JobService
extends java.lang.Object

    JobService provides interfaces to monitor submitted job.

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      static class  JobService.AbstractJobletObserver
      AbstractJobletObserver provides a mechanism to monitor the state of the submitted job.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      static Result cancelJob(android.content.Context context, java.lang.String jobId)
      Request to cancel a job with jobId.
      static JobInfo getJobInfo(android.content.Context context, java.lang.String jobId, Result result)
      Returns the JobInfo for the specified job ID synchronously.
      static boolean isSupported(android.content.Context context)
      This method is needed to determine if the service is supported or not.
      static java.lang.String monitorJobInForeground(android.app.Activity activity, java.lang.String jobId, JobletAttributes letAttributes, android.content.Intent pendingIntent)
      Monitors a job asynchronously for the specified jobId.

    • Method Detail

      • monitorJobInForeground

        public static java.lang.String monitorJobInForeground(android.app.Activity activity,
                                                      java.lang.String jobId,
                                                      JobletAttributes letAttributes,
                                                      android.content.Intent pendingIntent)

        Monitors a job asynchronously for the specified jobId. It enables providing an Activity for showing job progress dialog.

        Parameters:
        activity - The activity hosting job interaction dialogs.
        jobId - The jobId of the job to monitor.
        letAttributes - Attributes related to how the JobService should behave.
        pendingIntent - (Optional) Intent that will be sent as broadcast upon job completion. The extras passed as part of the intent as specified in the above table provide details about the job after its completion. It should be exported and security should be ensured by the client (e.g. provide explicit component in the intent, check jobId using Joblet.Keys.KEY_JOBID).
        Returns:
        String requested id which is used to correspond
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if activity or jobId is null
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if activity is destroyed or is finishing
        Since:
        API 1

      • cancelJob

        public static Result cancelJob(android.content.Context context,
                               java.lang.String jobId)

        Request to cancel a job with jobId.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context in which the application is running.
        jobId - Job ID for requesting cancel.
        Returns:
        Result Indicates the result whether job cancellation is succeeded or failed.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If context or jobId is null.
        Since:
        API 1

      • getJobInfo

        public static JobInfo getJobInfo(android.content.Context context,
                                 java.lang.String jobId,
                                 Result result)

        Returns the JobInfo for the specified job ID synchronously.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context in which the application is running.
        jobId - Job ID to get the details.
        result - (optional) Indicates any errors which occurred while retrieving the job info.
        Returns:
        JobInfo of the specified job.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if context or jobId is null
        Since:
        API 1

      • isSupported

        public static boolean isSupported(android.content.Context context)

        This method is needed to determine if the service is supported or not. If it's not supported, JobService operation will be failed.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context in which the application is running.
        Returns:
        boolean Returns result of supported. If service is supported, method returns true.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if context is null.
        Since:
        API 1
